agent_notes: Sutton produced a compact quality list, not 30-35 venues. The binding constraints were independent Books & Coffee scarcity, limited current-exhibition press, and sparse same-category backups within PAINTS walking radii.
Creative Walks are limited to Beddington Park and Nonsuch Park because both are on the PAINTS v6 Safety-First Shortlist; other Sutton parks were rejected or avoided.
Several venues need direct host confirmation for group walk-in/capacity and bank-holiday/event-date hours; unknowns are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.opening_hours: HappyCow lists Wed 10:00-17:30, Thu 10:00-23:30, Fri-Sat 10:00-00:00, Sun 11:00-21:00; official site/Instagram must be checked for event-date changes. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Public cafe/bar/music venue; walk-in bar/cafe access appears normal outside ticketed shows, but group 8-12 policy is not published. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable in daytime/early evening because Musicians Union lists 120 mixed seated/standing capacity; heads-up call needed for tables.
venues.capacity_notes: Musicians Union lists 120 mixed seated and standing capacity; ground-floor accessible venue.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu or Fri 17:00-19:00 before ticketed gig peak, or weekend late afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: A grassroots music venue, plant-based eatery, record-shop-adjacent community hub and carbon-neutral culture space gives attendees more to talk about than a standard pub. The venue’s story is Sutton-specific and resilient, with roots/music programming and community activity rather than generic nightlife. Strong PAINTS fit for show-and-tell prompts around records, gig posters, sustainability and local culture.
venues.anecdote_story: Founded in 2012 and now billed as the UK’s first carbon-neutral music venue; reporting and the venue history describe moves through several south London sites before settling in Sutton.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Plant-based food, local craft beer, bar/cafe offer, live roots music, vinyl/record culture and community events.
venues.safety_notes: High Street and rail-linked; evening arrivals should use Sutton station/main-lit routes. No park meeting point involved.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min on current evidence; closest PAINTS-coded cultural bar is CryerArts in Carshalton, about 35-45 min walk / short transit. Kept Tier A because venue itself is unusually robust but host should treat backup as transit-based.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketed performances may change bar availability; group walk-in and event-date hours need direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA and Instagram list Mon-Sat noon-23:00, Sun noon-22:30; official page says open daily and flags current beer festival/Sutton POTY 2026. Bank-holiday/event-date handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Traditional community pub; no reservation-only signal found. Group of 8-12 walk-in likely possible off-peak, but table policy is unpublished. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for conversational drinks if arriving early afternoon or early evening before peak; call ahead for one large table.
venues.capacity_notes: Community pub; capacity not published. CAMRA profile confirms regular pub opening and cask focus.
venues.best_time_slot: Sat 15:00-17:00 or Thu 18:00-20:00 outside lunch rush and late-evening crowd.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the strongest Sutton drinks pick: community-owned, much-awarded, intimate and deeply local. It has a real origin story, not a brand concept, and enough CAMRA/cultural proof to anchor a show-and-tell conversation around pubs as civic infrastructure. More ale-led than natural-wine-led, but the character and story compensate.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub was threatened with conversion before staff and customers raised money and took a free-of-tie lease in 2010; it has since become a repeat CAMRA London Pub of the Year winner.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Cask ale, beer festival programming, pub food and community-pub atmosphere rather than natural wine.
venues.safety_notes: Carshalton village setting near lit streets and rail/bus links; advise groups to leave together after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Peak beer-festival periods may be noisy/crowded; not natural wine, so PAINTS host should frame as community-pub culture.

venues.opening_hours: Spotlight site lists Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-20:00; older 39 High St page lists Mon-Thu 16:00-23:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-23:00, Sun 12:00-19:00. Treat as UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Restaurant/bar appears open to non-ticket holders, but event-night and group walk-in policy not published. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible in bar/restaurant at non-peak times; confirm no show-night private/event pressure.
venues.capacity_notes: CryerArts is a small theatre/community arts centre; public sources list theatre capacity around 68-125, but bar table capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Fri/Sat 12:00-16:00 or Sun afternoon; avoid immediately before big shows unless booked by host.
venues.why_fits_paints: A community arts centre bar gives Sutton a cultural drinks option that is more textured than a high-street chain. The building’s public hall/cinema/theatre lineage gives hosts a story to work with. It is Tier B because group walk-in mechanics and exact hours conflict across sources.
venues.anecdote_story: The building began as a 19th-century public hall, later had roller-skating and cinema uses, and reopened as a local arts venue after closure.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Coffee, cocktails, food, theatre/event audiences and local performance programming.
venues.safety_notes: Central Carshalton High Street location with rail/bus access; night exit should be via lit High Street routes.
venues.risks_watchouts: Private events/show nights can affect ambience and seating; hours need direct verification.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Wallington Old Town Hall / Wallington rail
venues.opening_hours: EcoLocal states second Saturday monthly, 09:00-13:00, with 2026 dates including 11 July, 8 August, 12 September, 10 October, 14 November and 12 December.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no booking needed for attendees. Meeting-point cafe/group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a wandering 8-12 group if the host uses a clear meeting point and keeps purchases/weather flexible.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor market around Old Town Hall and Library Gardens; Sutton Quakers cite established 1999 origin and around 28 local producer stalls.
venues.best_time_slot: Second Saturday, 10:00-12:00 after setup and before closing.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Sutton’s clearest recurring market pick: producer-led, local, small-scale and not a generic shopping-centre event. The 1999 origin gives it community depth. It supports PAINTS pacing because people can split into stall conversations and regroup easily.
venues.anecdote_story: EcoLocal runs the borough’s long-standing farmers’ market around the old civic buildings and Library Gardens, with local producer emphasis.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Fresh produce, artisan food, seasonal goods and local producer stalls.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime civic/high-street setting near Wallington station; weather exposure is the main operational issue.
venues.backup_venue: Sutton Craft and Farmers Market / Pop Up At The Pitch
venues.backup_walk_time: About 15 min by transit or short taxi; not a same-day assured backup because dates differ. Host must verify event calendar.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather-sensitive; no true same-day market backup within 15 min found in Sutton.

venues.opening_hours: 2026 trail listed Sat 27 Jun-Sun 5 Jul, 11:00-17:00; Friends of Honeywood also lists CAOS 27/28 June and 4/5 July. Seasonal event, not weekly/monthly.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open studios/free trail format; individual studios may have capacity pinch points. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with CAOS map/app and selected stops.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as a curated mini-route of 3-4 studios/anchors rather than whole trail; group may need to enter smaller studios in two waves.
venues.capacity_notes: Sutton Information Hub cites 80+ artists across 29 locations; capacity varies by studio.
venues.best_time_slot: Event weekends 11:30-14:30, starting at Honeywood or Carshalton village.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is closer to an art market/trail than a conventional market, but it is the most PAINTS-coded maker event in Sutton. It lets attendees meet artists, handle local craft and discuss process in non-institutional settings. Tier A for taste, with operational caveats because it is seasonal.
venues.anecdote_story: CAOS 2026 is its 10th year, spread from Carshalton Village up to Oaks Park with photography, ceramics and other local art forms.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Paintings, prints, ceramics, textiles, photography and cards in open studios and pop-up trail sites.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime village route; do not route through unapproved parks as Creative Walk content. Use streets and named venues only unless host validates route.
venues.backup_walk_time: Start/anchor venue; same programme, not an independent market backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Only useful on CAOS dates; studio-by-studio capacity and accessibility require route pre-check.

venues.venue_name: Beddington Park & The Grange Creative Walk
venues.opening_hours: Open public park; cafe listed by Sutton Information Hub as 7 days 09:00-16:30. Park-specific event-date restrictions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Park open-access. Weather backup Pavilion Cafe walk-in/group policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 in daylight; use clear meeting point and avoid after-dark routing.
venues.capacity_notes: 58-hectare park with formal gardens, lake, woodland walks and Wandle route; suitable for dispersed conversation.
venues.best_time_slot: Daylight late morning or early afternoon; avoid dusk/wet ground.
venues.why_fits_paints: Beddington Park is on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist, so it qualifies for Creative Walks. The Wandle, Grange Garden, lake and Carew Manor edges provide enough visual/storied stops for a 2-hour route. It gives Sutton an outer-London landscape pick without inventing unsafe routes.
venues.anecdote_story: Suggested stops: Grange Garden and Alfred Smee landscape layers; River Wandle chalk stream; restored lake/stock pond; Carew Manor/Beddington Place historic edge.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Designed landscape, river, formal gardens, lake, woodland and heritage architecture views.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist park. Daytime only; host should keep route to main paths and finish near cafe/transport before dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of start if meeting at cafe/Church Road side
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe hours and renovation status need direct confirmation; park is safety-first rather than premium, so keep it daylight.

venues.opening_hours: Open public park; Nonsuch Pantry Instagram says open 08:30-17:00 every day while HappyCow lists 08:30-16:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Park open-access. Nonsuch Pantry cafe appears walk-in, but group 8-12 seating policy unpublished. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in daylight; cafe may be small, so the host should not rely on indoor seating for all attendees.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public park around Nonsuch Mansion and former palace site; weather backup cafe is small/outdoor-oriented.
venues.best_time_slot: 10:30-12:30 or 13:00-15:00 daylight, beginning near Nonsuch Pantry/Mansion.
venues.why_fits_paints: Nonsuch Park is explicitly on the PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist and has the strongest story payload of any Sutton walk. The vanished Tudor palace gives attendees a vivid absence to discuss, while the mansion/parkland keeps the route visually legible. Tier A, with cafe capacity as the main caveat.
venues.anecdote_story: Suggested stops: Nonsuch Mansion; approximate palace site built for Henry VIII from 1538; surviving Little Park landscape; old tracks/tree avenues and the story of the palace being demolished in the 1680s.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Parkland, Nonsuch Mansion, former Tudor palace landscape and heritage interpretation opportunities.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS Safety-First Shortlist park. Daytime only; stay on main paths and start/end near Nonsuch Pantry or Mansion access.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min if route starts at Mansion/Pantry side
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe hours differ by source; exact palace-site interpretation should be prepared by host in advance.

venues.opening_hours: Sutton Heritage lists Thu-Fri-Sat 10:00-17:00; Friends cafe page lists tea room Thu-Sun 10:15-16:45 and Mon-Wed 10:15-15:45. Event-date/bank-holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: General admission free; public museum. Group 8-12 walk-in and room-use policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Museum rooms and tea room can support a small PAINTS group if paced carefully; Sutton room-hire page lists up to 20 for hired room use.
venues.capacity_notes: Room-hire capacity up to 20 during open hours; tea room seating capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Thu-Sat 11:00-13:00 or 14:00-16:00; on CAOS weekends use 11:30-14:30.
venues.why_fits_paints: Honeywood is a proper Sutton anchor: domestic scale, Carshalton Ponds setting, local-history texture and an independent-feeling tea-room rhythm. Current CAOS-related activity gives it an art/craft layer around the run date. It clears the bar as a museum/gallery day if the host confirms the specific display currently on view.
venues.anecdote_story: Honeywood includes a 17th-century flint/chalk core behind later fronts; Friends history notes a stream under the house and its position beside Carshalton Ponds.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local history displays, Carshalton material culture, shop/tea room; CAOS 2026 activity around Honeywood and local artists in late June/early July.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Carshalton Ponds setting; do not use nearby Grove Park as a Creative Walk substitute because it is not on PAINTS safety list.
venues.risks_watchouts: Museum/gallery criterion is exhibition-sensitive; current show details are local rather than major press-backed, so host must verify what is on view.

venues.opening_hours: Sutton Heritage lists Thu-Fri 10:00-16:00 and Sat 12:00-16:00; page warns hours may occasionally vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date status.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free general-admission historic house; group 8-12 walk-in/tour policy not published. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable as a slow museum visit or booked tour; small historic-house spaces may require staggered movement.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house/museum in Cheam Village; capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: A Grade II* Tudor house in Cheam gives Sutton a compact heritage pick with a real building story. It is more history-house than current-exhibition gallery, which caps it at Tier B. Good backup or quieter alternative when Honeywood is unavailable.
venues.anecdote_story: Built around 1500 and opened as a local museum in 1978 after a community campaign; it preserves Sutton heritage and local memory.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, local heritage displays and periodic tours/events.
venues.safety_notes: Cheam Village daytime setting; check post-visit route to Cheam station after dark if using late opening/events.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 20-25 min walk; no qualifying museum/gallery backup within 10 min found, so Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: No recent/current exhibition press found; tour availability and group policy need direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Sutton event page lists Little Holland House Open Day 2026 - Summer on 5 July 2026 with hourly slots 11:00-16:00; general page says first Sunday monthly/open days via events. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm availability before programming.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open days/tours appear slot-based; group walk-in not assured. Reservation/timed-entry risk caps at Tier B.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best only with a pre-confirmed group tour or timed slots; not reliable as a spontaneous matched event.
venues.capacity_notes: Small Arts and Crafts house; capacity not published.
venues.best_time_slot: First Sunday open day, 11:00-13:00, only with confirmed slot availability.
venues.why_fits_paints: A tiny Arts and Crafts house made by artist-craftsman Frank Dickinson is almost absurdly PAINTS-coded for object-led conversation. The issue is operational: open days are limited and likely tour/slot constrained. Tier B despite high aesthetic/story value.
venues.anecdote_story: Built 1902-1904 by Frank R. Dickinson and Florence Dickinson; Sutton Heritage says most of what is on display, from foundations to furnishings, was created through their partnership.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Arts and Crafts interiors, furniture/furnishings and domestic craft environment.
venues.safety_notes: Residential Carshalton Beeches setting; use daylight slots and pre-arranged travel guidance.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 25-30 min walk / short transit; no qualifying 10-min museum/gallery backup found, so Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited open days, slot availability and group capacity are the binding constraints.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Only clear bookshop-cafe option found in Sutton, but Waterstones/Café W is a national chain, which PAINTS v6 hard-rejects for Tier A and does not satisfy the independent Books & Coffee requirement.
Independent coffee signal exists, but no substantive books/bookshop component found, so it fails the raised Books & Coffee category rule.
Attractive arts/cinema/cafe-bar concept, but CAMRA flags the pub as closed long term/seeking tenants; conflicting sources make operational status too risky for inclusion.
Charming independent pub candidate, but category fit is weaker than The Hope/Sound Lounge/CryerArts: limited cultural/owner-story evidence and no clear walk-in/group policy source.
Visually tempting near Honeywood, but Grove Park is not on the PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list. It can be incidental scenery for a museum visit, not a sourced Creative Walk.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Mellows Park / Oaks Park / The Grove
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Local parks/open spaces with possible appeal, but absent from PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list, so not sourced.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Sutton Craft and Farmers Market at Sutton United
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Good local maker-market texture, but the official event page found was a passed February 2026 event rather than a stable upcoming recurring market calendar; retained only as loose backup lead.
Excellent-looking independent wine bar/shop, but Chipstead is outside the London Borough of Sutton target geography for this district file.
